page: Amazon Web Services r7i.12xlarge is a memory-optimized virtual server instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores based on the x86_64 architecture, utilizing Intel Xeon 8488C processors running at 3.2 GHz. The instance is equipped with 384.0 GB of DDR5 RAM operating at 5 GHz, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying on network-attached storage and offering a baseline network bandwidth of 19 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate strong performance in memory-intensive and computational tasks. The instance achieves a read-only memory bandwidth of 3917.41 GB/sec for small cached blocks and 181.19 GB/sec for large uncached blocks, placing it in the top tier of memory performance. CPU capabilities are highlighted by a Passmark CPU score of 58891.69 and strong Geekbench multi-core scores of 17914.0. Additionally, the hardware supports Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and AVX-512, which contribute to strong prompt processing speeds in LLM inference benchmarks, such as 237.33 tokens/sec for a medium 7B model.

From a resource density perspective, the r7i.12xlarge offers a highly efficient profile for workloads requiring large memory footprints without the overhead of dedicated physical hardware. The use of DDR5 memory and high-speed Intel Xeon processors delivers strong memory bandwidth and database operation performance, as evidenced by a Passmark database score of 22288.25. However, the lack of local NVMe storage means all storage operations must traverse the network, which may introduce latency tradeoffs compared to storage-optimized instances with local drives.

This instance is designed for memory-intensive enterprise workloads. It is highly suitable for large-scale relational and non-relational databases, in-memory caches, data analytics, and software compilation. The inclusion of specialized Intel instruction sets also makes it a viable option for CPU-based machine learning inference and cryptographic processing.
description: Amazon Web Services r7i.12xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores powered by Intel Xeon 8488C processors at 3.2 GHz. Built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, it provides 384.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 RAM at 5 GHz, yielding 8.0 GB of memory per core. The instance lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs, utilizing a 19 Gbps baseline network connection instead. Performance benchmarks show strong capabilities, particularly in memory bandwidth, database operations, and CPU-based LLM prompt processing, supported by Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX). This profile makes the r7i.12xlarge highly efficient for memory-intensive workloads. It is best suited for large databases, in-memory caching, data analytics, and enterprise applications requiring substantial memory capacity and strong multi-core processing without local storage requirements.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r7i.12xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 48 vCPUs, 384 GB of DDR5 RAM, and Intel Xeon processors, designed for high-performance database and memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r7i.12xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 48 vCPUs, 384 GB DDR5 RAM, and Intel Xeon processors for enterprise workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring high-speed DDR5 RAM and advanced Intel Xeon processors for demanding database and analytical workloads.
bullet_points: 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores powered by Intel Xeon 8488C processors
384 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory
Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and AVX-512 support for accelerated computing
19 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with Nitro hypervisor integration
Optimized for large-scale databases, in-memory caching, and data analytics
